267020: The Dri-FIT Classic is the Nike polo that built the corporate golf category — nearly 500 have gone through our embroidery heads. The swoosh on the sleeve does the brand work; your logo on the chest does yours. It's the safe-and-sharp pick for client gifts, sales teams, and tournament orders.
102699: The Heritage 86 is Nike's soft, unstructured cap — the relaxed silhouette that reads athletic without the stiff crown. We ran over 400 in a single order for one customer, and the low-profile front takes a small embroidered mark exactly the way this cap wants: understated, with the swoosh on the side doing the rest.
637167: The Dri-FIT Vertical Mesh is the breathable workhorse of Nike's polo line — 40-plus separate orders through our shop, mostly companies that live in polos all summer. The vertical mesh texture hides wear, the wicking does its job, and the swoosh earns the price difference over a no-name equivalent.
NKFQ4761: The Nike Pro Hooded Jacket is the athletic-side outerwear pick — a technical hooded layer with the swoosh credibility that makes it a coveted staff gift. We've embroidered them for sales teams and gym staff alike; the chest panel takes a logo cleanly next to the Nike mark.
NKDC1991: The Women's Dri-FIT Micro Pique 2.0 is the tailored half of Nike's most-popular corporate polo — 70 separate orders tell us companies genuinely pair it with the unisex version instead of ordering men's smalls. Same fine pique, same wicking, real women's fit, matching embroidery.
NKAH6266: The Dri-FIT Hex Textured Polo is Nike's step-up fabric — a subtle hex weave that photographs rich and feels tech. It's the polo for the leadership tier of a program: same brand, noticeably elevated hand, embroiders beautifully.
NKDR1513: The Club Fleece Full-Zip Hoodie brings the swoosh to company casual — Nike's soft everyday fleece with the sleeve swoosh crews genuinely want. It's a staff-gift regular here; a left-chest embroidered logo rides alongside the brand without fighting it.
799802: The Dri-FIT Players polo in Nike's Modern Fit — trimmed through the body for the golf-shop look without going slim. Twenty separate orders, mostly exec and client-gift tiers. The smooth face takes fine embroidery detail well.
NKDX6718: The Club Fleece Sleeve Swoosh 1/2-Zip is the quarter-zip everyone actually wants — Nike fleece, collar-up style, swoosh on the sleeve. Fifty-plus separate orders in crew-gift quantities; your embroidered logo on the chest completes it.
NKDC2108: The Dri-FIT Vapor is Nike's premium performance polo — lighter and faster-drying than the Micro Pique, with a price that says leadership tier. Twenty-five orders through our shop, mostly management upgrades on existing Nike programs.
746099: The Dri-FIT Solid Icon Pique in Modern Fit is Nike's refined classic — traditional pique texture, contemporary cut. It slots between the Classic and the Vapor in a program: familiar look, sharper fit, same swoosh equity.
NKBQ5231: The Dri-FIT Cotton/Poly Tee is the Nike t-shirt for teams — cotton comfort with wicking built in and the swoosh at the chest. Two hundred-plus decorated here; it's the athletic upgrade pick for staff shirts and event crews.
NKDC2114: The Dri-FIT Vapor Block colorblocks Nike's premium performance polo — a sportier look for teams that want the tech and some design. It shows up in our tournament and event orders where matching sets matter.
883681: The Dri-FIT Legacy is the quiet professional of Nike's polo range — clean, matte, dependable. Twenty-five separate orders, many of them re-orders, which is the truest compliment a uniform polo gets.
LOG101: The OGIO Jewel is the women's companion to our most-embroidered OGIO polo — nearly 200 decorated across a steady stream of orders. Modern stretch knit, a genuinely tailored cut, and colors that match the men's Caliber2.0 so mixed teams coordinate without compromise.
OG604: The OGIO Performance Cap matches the brand's polos in fabric and attitude — a structured performance hat with a face that takes embroidery sharply. We ran 200 in one batch for a single company; it's the cap for teams already wearing OGIO on their backs.
OG152: The OGIO Motion Polo is built around stretch — a performance knit that moves with sales calls, site walks, and golf swings alike. It's the modern-cut option in our OGIO program; 150 went out in one recent company order.
OG102: The OGIO Accelerator carries the brand's signature blend of tech fabric and design edge — a polo that reads sharper than corporate standard-issue. Companies pick it when they want uniforms that don't look like uniforms; the knit embroiders cleanly.
411092: The OGIO Logan Pack is the corporate-gift backpack that doesn't look corporate — padded laptop protection in OGIO's signature styling. We embroidered a 150-piece batch for one company's program; the front panel carries a logo like it was designed to.
OE720: The OGIO Crux Soft Shell is the brand's technical jacket — stretch, weather resistance, and a cut that works over a polo at the office or on the course. Eighteen separate orders and climbing; it's the modern alternative to the standard company soft shell.
OG143: The OGIO Slate Polo is the understated one — a matte, refined face in the brand's performance knit. Twenty-five separate orders says companies keep coming back to it as their everyday standard. Embroiders with excellent detail.
OG125: The OGIO Framework Polo brings subtle texture to the brand's performance line — visual interest that stays professional. It's a program polo for companies that want their team recognizably sharper than the competition's.
411066: The OGIO Wheelie Pack rolls and carries — a wheeled pack for travel-heavy teams that still fits an overhead bin. A niche piece with a loyal following in our client-gift orders; the panel takes a clean embroidered mark.
OG170: The OGIO Regain Polo is the brand's recycled-content performance polo — sustainability story included, OGIO look intact. Companies with environmental commitments order it to walk the talk in their uniform program.
411073: The OGIO Surge RSS Pack is the everyday tech backpack of the line — laptop-armored, cable-organized, and 19 of its 21 units went out in separate orders: it's a one-at-a-time executive gift that keeps getting picked.
411061: The OGIO Ace Pack is the sleeker, lighter sibling in the brand's pack line — everyday carry without the bulk. Companies order small logoed batches for new-hire kits with a laptop inside.
OE701: The OGIO Fulcrum 1/4-Zip is the layering piece of the brand's technical line — a stretch quarter-zip that works over their polos and under their shells. Thirteen orders through our shop; the chest takes embroidery cleanly.
OE721: The OGIO Pivot Soft Shell earned one of the most telling stats in our books — 24 of its 29 units sold in separate orders. That's an executive-gift pattern: bought one at a time, on purpose, repeatedly. Modern cut, technical fabric, sharp with a small embroidered mark.
TM1MW452: The Crestview 1/4-Zip is TravisMathew's signature piece and our best-selling item from the brand — nearly 200 through the shop across 48 orders. It's the famously soft quarter-zip that executives buy once and then order for the whole leadership team. A small tone-on-tone embroidered logo is the move.
TM1MU411: The Oceanside Solid is TravisMathew's core polo — the coastal-soft knit that made the brand a client-gift phenomenon. Seventy-four separate orders is the most reorder-dense pattern in our premium lineup; people feel this one and come back. Embroider it subtle and let the hand speak.
TM1MU426: The Rad Flexback is TravisMathew's fitted performance cap — stretch-fit comfort with the brand's premium finish. We ran 370 across a handful of big orders; it's the cap for companies whose swag has a reputation to keep. The front panel takes clean, tight embroidery.
TM1MU423: The Cruz Trucker is TravisMathew's take on the mesh-back — coastal styling, premium materials, nothing like a giveaway hat. Nearly 300 decorated here; it's the trucker for brands that want the relaxed look without the promo-product feel.
TM1MY390: The Cruz Colorblock Trucker adds two-tone styling to TravisMathew's popular mesh-back — a cap with some design to it, still premium to the touch. Companies order it when the plain version feels too quiet; embroidery or a patch both work on the front panel.
TM1MU414: The Oceanside Blocked brings colorblock design to TravisMathew's signature soft polo — same coveted hand, more visual interest. Twenty-seven separate orders, mostly teams that wanted their program one notch bolder than solids.
TM1WW003: The Women's Crestview 1/4-Zip is the tailored cut of TravisMathew's flagship — the same famously soft fabric, fitted properly. Companies gifting the Crestview order this alongside it so the whole leadership team gets the real thing.
TM1MY402: The Coastal Chest Stripe polo carries TravisMathew's soft-knit signature with a single chest stripe — 37 separate orders, which for a premium polo means it's being picked deliberately, over and over. Embroidery rides clean above or opposite the stripe.
TM1MY400: The Coto Performance Chest Stripe is the athletic end of TravisMathew's polo range — performance fabric, one confident stripe. It's a tournament-and-client-gift regular; the knit takes fine embroidered detail well.
TM1MW450: The Monterey Chest Stripe polo is classic TravisMathew styling — coastal softness, a single retro stripe, golf-shop finish. Companies use it as the elevated tier of a polo program; a small logo keeps the balance right.
TM1MW451: The Auckland Slub polo brings texture to TravisMathew's lineup — a slub knit that reads premium casual rather than uniform. Twenty-seven separate orders, almost all small-batch: it's the polo people choose for themselves and the gift-givers follow.
TM1MU416: The Cabana Chest Stripe rounds out TravisMathew's stripe family — laid-back coastal styling over the brand's signature soft hand. It shows up in our exec-gift and golf-event orders; subtle embroidery finishes it properly.
